exemplar.title: To what extent have Primeâs marketing strategies led to its success?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have Primeâs marketing strategies led to its success?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and consistently linked to the analysis throughout the essay.
The introduction and theoretical framing effectively communicate the topic and scope, using relevant Business Management frameworks.
The topic is communicated accurately and coherently, with comprehensive background on Prime and justification of its significance.
A generally appropriate range of internal and external sources is used, and initial evaluation of relevance and limitations is demonstrated in the source table.
weaknesses: Source selection shows uneven academic rigor, relying in places on journalistic or anecdotal materials without systematic triangulation.
Transitions back to the research question (e.g., when introducing Ansoffâs Matrix) are sometimes less explicit, weakening the focus.
The conclusion stops short of giving a definitive statement on the extent to which marketing strategies drove Primeâs success.
Methodology description lacks depth on how sources were critically evaluated for reliability and bias.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band in Focus and Method: the research question is clear and sustained, the topic is communicated effectively, and an appropriate range of sources is used with some evidence of selection. Minor lapses in source rigor and occasional lapses in linking back to the question prevent a maximum score, so 5/6 is awarded.

strengths: Business Management terminology (e.g., cost leadership, USP, product life-cycle) is used accurately and consistently.
Quantitative data (sales figures, ad costs, market share) are integrated with theory, demonstrating relevant application of source material.
Figures and strategic diagrams (Ansoffâs Matrix, Porterâs Generic Strategies) are correctly presented and labeled, showing clear conceptual understanding.
Subject knowledge of strategic and ethical marketing concepts is clear and coherent throughout the discussion.
weaknesses: Several key data points rely on non-peer-reviewed blogs or secondary commentary without triangulation from primary financial reports.
Some claims (e.g., 50% sales decline) lack precise citation, reducing transparency of evidence.
Charts and tables lack brief notes on data collection methods or margins of error, limiting critical appraisal of source reliability.
The ownership details and FDA guideline references depend on a single secondary source and would benefit from more authoritative documentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear and coherent subject knowledge and mostly relevant application of source material, aligning with the upper range of the 3â4 band. Occasional reliance on weak sources and incomplete citations prevent higher performance, so 4/6 is appropriate.

strengths: The analysis employs multiple strategic frameworks (Porter, Ansoff, Blue/Red Ocean) with strong connection to evidence and clear, well-supported conclusions.
Ethical critiques (e.g., marketing to minors) are sophisticated and demonstrate deep critical evaluation of real-world consequences.
Recommendations for extension strategies and corrective marketing actions are insightful, feasible and grounded in theory.
Quantitative benchmarks (production costs, sponsorship spend) are used to support nuanced discussion of competitive positioning and ROI considerations.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes findings, reinforcing the overall argument and reflecting on both successes and limitations of Primeâs strategies.
weaknesses: The critique of tool limitations (e.g., Porterâs model) is only briefly mentioned and could be expanded to deepen evaluation.
Long-term ROI of sponsorships and projected payback analyses are discussed descriptively but lack quantitative projections.
The methodology section does not detail how source reliability and bias were systematically assessed.
Some assertions about competitor behavior and market dynamics are made without full quantitative validation or risk gradation analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work falls solidly within the upper end of the 7â9 band for Critical Thinking: research is consistently relevant, analysis is sophisticated and well supported, and evaluation is critical. A few missed opportunities to deepen critique and quantify long-term impacts prevent a move to the 10â12 band, so 9/12 is awarded.

strengths: The report follows the required IB structure (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references).
Page numbering, headings and sub-headings are consistently applied, aiding navigation and clarity.
Tables, figures and diagrams are labeled and referenced appropriately within the text.
Overall formatting is neat, with a logical progression of sections and clear layout conventions.
weaknesses: Citation style is not fully consistent; reference entries vary in completeness and formatting.
Duplicate entries and missing access dates appear in the works cited, undermining bibliographic clarity.
Figure captions lack photographer/designer credits and consistent citation format.
Some lengthy paragraphs and occasional repetition reduce visual clarity and conciseness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the essential structural and formatting requirements, placing it in the 3â4 band. However, inconsistencies in citation formatting, reference list errors and occasional layout issues prevent a top-band score, resulting in 3/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââTo what extent have Primeâs marketing strategies led to its success?ââis sharply focused, open-ended and directly linked to Business Management concepts of performance and promotion.  The body of the essay repeatedly returns to the success dimension (revenue, market ranking, brand image) and to the causal role of specific marketing actions, showing effective connection.  Although the question could have been narrowed further (for example by defining success more precisely or delimiting time period), the stated focus is clear and sufficiently tight to guide coherent analysis.

The discussion draws balanced attention to both the successes (rapid revenue growth) and shortcomings (ethical backlash, sales decline) of Primeâs marketing.  The student weighs benefits against risks and suggests feasible strategic responses, showing partial critical evaluation.  Still, the depth of critique and reflection on alternative perspectives could be stronger, positioning the work at the top of the 4-6 band.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of Business Management concepts: Porterâs strategies, economies of scale, product life-cycle, scarcity marketing and ethical marketing are explained accurately and applied to Prime.  Explanatory depth is coherent, though occasionally descriptive, and some theoretical limitations are recognised.  The understanding is clear but not outstandingly nuanced, matching the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Business terminology such as âcost leadership,â âUSP,â âextension strategies,â and ânegative image associationâ is used correctly and consistently.  Occasional informal phrasing (âblew up,â âhypeâ) appears, yet does not materially detract from conceptual precision.  Overall, the work shows mostly accurate, appropriate use of specialised vocabulary, fulfilling the 3-4 band.

Analytical sections employ multiple strategic tools to dissect Primeâs positioning, cost structure and ethical issues.  The student connects theory to evidence, contrasts Prime with competitors, and evaluates the implications of different strategies.  Conclusions about the interplay of low-cost and differentiation, and the mis-targeting of PRIME Energy, reveal clear, well-supported reasoning, although some assertions lack depth of quantitative validation.  This aligns with the lower end of the 7-9 âgood, well-supported analysisâ band.

Source material is integrated into the narrativeâsales figures, cost estimates, sponsorship feesâbut citations occasionally rely on secondary reporting or YouTube commentary, and some claims (e.g., production cost comparisons) are weakly supported.  Nevertheless, most evidence is pertinent to the arguments being advanced, fulfilling the descriptor of âmostly relevant and appropriate application,â situated in the 3-4 band.

A range of internal and external sources is used (company website, Statista, Forbes, YouTube interviews) and the methodology section briefly justifies tool and source selection.  However, the list remains uneven in academic rigorâseveral sources are journalistic or anecdotalâand financial data are acknowledged as approximate.  While each source is at least loosely relevant, evidence of systematic, critically informed selection (e.g., triangulation, primary data collection) is limited.  Consequently the work aligns with the middle band where sources are âgenerally relevantâ¦with some evidence of informed selection.â

The essay assembles a reasonable breadth of quantitative and qualitative dataârevenue figures, cost estimates, sales rankings, stakeholder statementsâand links them to the research question.  While credibility of some numbers is questioned by the student, the selection remains generally relevant and sufficient to support the inquiry, fitting the mid-range (4-6) descriptor of âadequate research clearly relevant to the research question.â

Page numbers, title, and word count are provided; tables and figures are labelled; overall formatting is neat.  Citation style is present but not always fully consistent, and some image attributions lack full bibliographic detail.  Nonetheless, the main layout conventions are observed, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band, albeit at the lower end due to inconsistencies.

The topicâPrimeâs success in relation to its marketing strategiesâis stated explicitly in the title, reiterated in the introduction, and consistently referred back to throughout the essay.  The student provides sufficient contextual background on the company, explains why the issue is worth examining, and outlines the theoretical lenses (7Ps, Porter, Ansoff, Blue/Red Ocean) that will be used.  This clear framing and sustained linkage to the research focus enable the reader to understand both scope and direction of the investigation.  Minor lapses in phrasing and occasional repetition prevent the communication from being fully comprehensive, but overall the explanation is accurate and effective, warranting placement in the 5-6 band.

The essay follows the required IB EE structure with title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion and works cited.  Headings, sub-headings, tables and figures are used effectively, and the argument progresses logically.  Minor issues (e.g., some lengthy paragraphs, occasional repetition) exist but do not undermine clarity.  These qualities meet the criteria for a âclearly appropriate structure,â meriting 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The Table of Contents is well structured and clearly lists all major sections, supporting easy navigation of the essay.
The introduction effectively frames the topic, provides essential background on Prime, and links context to the research question, ensuring the reader understands scope and purpose.
The title page clearly presents the investigation title and author, fulfilling the requirement for a properly formatted title page.
The research question is prominently stated, open-ended and directly linked to the investigation focus, providing clear direction for the analysis.
A word count is provided at the start of the report, meeting the formal requirement for Extended Essays.
The table of key sources clearly links each to its purpose and limitations, demonstrating thoughtful initial evaluation of relevance.
Terminology in the Red Ocean vs Blue Ocean section is consistently accurate and demonstrates clear understanding of core concepts.
The analysis applies the tool descriptively but does not critically evaluate its limitations in assessing competitive positioning; an explicit critique here would deepen analysis.
The recommendations for extension strategies are insightful and grounded in theory, demonstrating strong evaluative reasoning.
The transition into using Ansoffâs Matrix could more explicitly link back to the original research question to maintain tight focus.
The Ansoffâs Matrix diagram is clearly presented and correctly labeled, demonstrating accurate use of strategic planning concepts.

The reliance on a single secondary source (âopkisc, 2024â) for company ownership details limits the depth of source application; consider triangulating with a corporate registry or financial report.
The methodology outlines source selection but lacks details on how reliability and bias were systematically assessed; describing specific evaluation criteria would strengthen credibility.
Work cited headings lack consistent formatting (e.g., font size, indentation); standardize using a single citation style guide throughout.
Citation entries vary in completeness and structure (e.g., missing authors or access dates); ensure uniform formatting for all references.
The discussion integrates quantitative data (follower counts, Super Bowl ad costs) with theory, indicating relevant and appropriate application of source material.
The consideration of sponsorship costs introduces a challenge but lacks deeper evaluation of long-term ROI; adding projected payback analysis would enhance critical insight.
The Porterâs Generic Strategies matrix is accurately reproduced, reflecting a clear understanding of how scope and competitive advantage interrelate.
The analysis notes a limitation of Porterâs model but does not explore how Primeâs integrated strategy might manifest in specific marketing actions; elaboration would strengthen evaluation.
The cost leadership discussion uses precise production figures and industry benchmarks, showing sophisticated analysis depth and well-supported conclusions.
Sources for cost figures are from non-peer-reviewed blogs; incorporating data from audited financial statements or industry reports would improve source reliability.
The USP analysis demonstrates clear subject knowledge by linking product attributes to differentiation strategy in consumer health trends.
Figure captions lack photographer or designer credit and inconsistent citation format; ensure all visuals include full bibliographic details.
The inclusion of a Statista chart effectively contextualizes Primeâs market position against major competitors, enhancing research quality.
The chart data are presented without discussing potential margins of error or data collection methods; a brief note on data source methodology is advised.
The application of the product life cycle model to Primeâs sales trend is conceptually accurate and demonstrates correct use of theoretical frameworks.
The 50% sales decline claim is not accompanied by a direct citation of the data source; specifying the exact reference would improve source transparency.
The concluding statement does not explicitly state to what extent marketing strategies led to Primeâs success; a more definitive answer would close the inquiry effectively.
Duplicate entries appear in the reference list (e.g., Forbes Health listed twice); remove redundancies to enhance presentation clarity.
The analysis applies product development strategy but omits a discussion of risk gradations as implied by the model; integrating this would deepen evaluation.
The ethical critique of marketing PRIME Energy to minors reflects sophisticated discussion and evaluation of real-world consequences.
The FDA guideline reference would benefit from precise citation of the regulatory document or webpage to strengthen subject knowledge accuracy.
The corrective recommendations for PRIME Energyâs marketing are practical and directly address the identified ethical and demographic issues.
The reflection on methodology limitations and potential biases is limited; a more explicit acknowledgment of research constraints would demonstrate deeper engagement.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes findings on marketing impact and tool application, reinforcing the strength of the overall argument.
